summary: End-to-end testing of Acute Connect Crisis Kanban: 15 backend pytest tests (REST + WebSocket) all PASS; frontend smoke + integration tests via Playwright PASS for board load, KPI, REALTIME indicator, search filter, drawer edit + save, add patient dialog, and reseed. Drag-and-drop UI not exercised via Playwright (per instructions) but verified via PATCH /api/patients/{id}/move broadcast over websocket in pytest.

ui_bugs.issue: React 'Encountered two children with the same key' warning observed after creating a patient (and again after reseed). Race condition: onCreatePatient does setPatients((prev) => [...prev, res.data]) WITHOUT dedup, while the WS 'patient.created' broadcast can arrive concurrently and add the same id (its handler does dedup, but the create handler doesn't). Recommend deduping in onCreatePatient: prev.some(p => p.id === res.data.id) ? prev : [...prev, res.data].
Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for DialogContent'. Add SheetDescription/DialogDescription for a11y.

action_items: Fix duplicate-key React warning in onCreatePatient by deduping by id (KanbanBoard.jsx).
Add Dialog/Sheet Description for a11y warnings (optional).
critical_code_review_comments: onCreatePatient lacks idempotency guard while WS broadcasts the same created patient -> duplicate key warning under race.
/api/patients/seed delete_many followed by insert_many is not atomic; broadcasts a single 'patients.seeded' event but does not emit individual deletes; clients fully refetch which is fine.
No request-size/limit validation on PatientCreate (e.g. age, risk_score bounds beyond 0-100 enforcement client-side).
WS endpoint @app.websocket('/api/ws') is mounted on app, not router, so prefix duplication is correctly avoided; reconnection logic is in the hook; verified.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass; minor warning only)

seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 12 demo patients; tests call POST /api/patients/seed?reset=true to guarantee a clean state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is at /app/backend/tests/test_kanban_api.py and covers REST + WebSocket. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Frontend live URL is in REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All data-testids documented in original review_request work as expected. Only frontend issue is a non-blocking React duplicate-key warning during patient creation (race between axios resolve and WS broadcast).
rca_of_the_issue: Duplicate key warning RCA: onCreatePatient (KanbanBoard.jsx line ~226) appends res.data unconditionally; meanwhile WebSocket broadcast 'patient.created' triggered by the POST also fires onWsEvent which does dedupe. If WS event lands BEFORE axios resolves, list contains the patient; the unconditional append then creates a duplicate React child. Fix: dedupe in onCreatePatient or rely solely on WS for state mutation.
